title: parkia arborea
intro: Parkia arborea, also known as the African Locust Bean or Igba, is a tropical tree native to West and Central Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seed, which contains a high concentration of tannins and other compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The seed's medicinal properties make it useful for treating various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, with health benefits including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and boosted immune system function. Herbal preparations of Parkia arborea include the seed's extraction into oils, creams, and infusions, which can be used for topical application or taken internally as a supplement.
